description_long: This object is a weathered stone grave monument featuring a rectangular base with detailed relief carvings on its sides. The base supports two upright, rounded headstones, each showing signs of age and erosion, with one displaying a recessed area that may have once held an inscription or decorative element. The reliefs on the sides depict figures and possibly animals, suggesting historical or symbolic significance. The surface is rough and uneven, with visible chips and discoloration from exposure to the elements. A small plaque or marker is present at the base of one headstone, though its text is not legible. The overall structure is solid and heavy, typical of traditional grave markers found in older cemeteries.
description: A weathered stone grave monument with two upright headstones and relief carvings on a rectangular base, showing signs of age and erosion.
description_view_0: Front view showing two upright headstones, one with a recessed area and a small plaque at its base, with detailed relief carvings visible on the front panel.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the relief carvings on the left panel and the thickness of the headstones, with the plaque and recessed area still partially visible.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headstones and the relief carvings on the rear panel, with less detail visible compared to the front.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the right panel's relief carvings and the solid, blocky construction of the monument.
